title: Open iEEG Dataset (Pediatric iEEG, Wayne State University and UCLA)

description: This dataset comprises de-identified interictal intracranial EEG (iEEG) recordings during sleep from 185 pediatric subjects with epilepsy, collected from UCLA Mattel Children's Hospital and Children's Hospital of Michigan. The dataset includes raw iEEG data with anatomical labels and clinical outcomes, along with processed derivatives containing high-frequency oscillation (HFO) detections using RMS and MNI methods. This resource supports research on pathological oscillations, epileptogenic zone characterization, and seizure prediction biomarkers.
methods_description: Intracranial EEG recordings were collected during sleep from pediatric epilepsy patients at two institutions. Processed derivatives include HFO detection using two methods (RMS and MNI detectors) and bipolar iEEG data. Channel-level anatomical labels and resection status information are provided for each subject.
